inline_comments.message:
Have you discussed with APP-C team to let them know that they can no longer poll Nova for status information? Have they agreed to this change?

For the plan of record, this email thread:

https://lists.onap.org/pipermail/onap-discuss/2018-January/007502.html

shows us that APP-C PTL has already communicated with our MultiVIM PTL that APP-C does NOT plan to use DMaaP for Beijing Release.

Do you mean you just change the language? Or will you make sure that APPC will continue to interact with MultiVIM in the same way that is already supported in Amsterdam so that APPC's capability in the entire ONAP platform will not be impacted?
I think this feature will not change anything in APPC, at least in this release, it only provides another option for them.

Hi Bin Sun, thanks for the updated design spec. Appreciate your effort. 
I do believe we need such event federation , and I can understand how the publisher works, but I cannot figure out how the listener is working. With the impression I had from previous discussion, the listener is to listen oslo event from openstack. My concern is that: Is that reasonable or even possible for multicloud (which is part ONAP hence outside of OpenStack) to listen oslo events which are supposed to be protected by security rules of VIM, or even unreachable due to network connectivity? 
Please correct me if I got your point wrong. Thanks
we will provide another ways to get the events in case of security rules or network issues, will update in patch set 4

Looks good clarification. Can we more explicitly ensure there is no impact on APPC? Consider something like:

"This design will ensure backward compatibility. Northbound components, e.g. APP-C, VF-C etc., can choose to continue their current way of receiving VM status/events check and all of the lifecycle management operations that they currently support in Amsterdam release. Their current capability and operation will not be impacted without having to use this feature."

Thank you
Bin

Because Listener listens to the events from VM, the questions are:

- what type of "publisher" do we anticipate in the VIM? In another word:
  * shall we reuse current mechanism of VIM to publish those events for Listener to capture?
  * Or shall we need to install/change/revise current VIM in order for VIM to publish events?
- what about public clouds such as Azure, Amazon etc?
Will this Publisher be implemented in VIM (e.g. OpenStack, or Azure) or somewhere else?

Sorry Bin Sun, it is still not clear if those REST APIs are existing APIs or new APIs. Can you add the paragraph, which I suggested in Patchset #3, to ensure that APP-C will not be impacted without having to add anything new?
Is it a new API or existing API?

If I understand, this is the rest api which called by multicloud plugin for all VIM/cloud which cannot expose oslo event to listener.
The APP-C should not be impacted since APP-C can still call the existing API which implemented in Amsterdam Release.
I suggest Bin Sun just put these statement in the design to eliminate these concerns.
